title: Bulbophyllum striatum
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum striatum is classified under the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. This classification is further divided into the subfamily Epidendroideae, and then into the tribe Malaxideae. Within the tribe Malaxideae, Bulbophyllum striatum is placed in the subtribe Malaxidinae. The species Bulbophyllum striatum belongs to the genus Bulbophyllum, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The classification of Bulbophyllum striatum reflects its unique characteristics and relationships to other orchid species.

intro: Bulbophyllum striatum, a species of orchid, is a type of flowering plant that grows in tropical regions, characterized by its unique, striated pseudobulbs and white to yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
